level.name: Augmented
level.tagline: AI is part of the standard workflow
level.description: AI is part of the team's standard workflow. Shared agent guidance exists and guardrails are solid (real test suite, CI gates, enforced types/lint), so AI-generated code is safe to merge.

posture.label: Solid but Manual
posture.blurb: Strong engineering foundations with untapped AI leverage — operationalize AI tooling.

dimensions.name: AI Tooling & Conventions
dimensions.summary: AI Tooling & Conventions: no supporting signals detected in the sampled repository.
evidence: No machine-readable AI/agent guidance detected (e.g. CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules)
gaps: Below target for an AI-native workflow. Is AI development operationalized with shared, machine-readable guidance?

dimensions.name: Automated Testing
dimensions.summary: Automated Testing: found 128 test files; test framework configured; end-to-end tests configured.
gaps: Already strong — focus on consistency and enforcement.

dimensions.name: CI/CD & Delivery
dimensions.summary: CI/CD & Delivery: github actions ci present; multiple ci workflows (5); ci runs tests.
gaps: Below target for an AI-native workflow. Automated pipelines, merge gates, release automation, and how code reaches production.

dimensions.name: Agentic Workflows
dimensions.summary: Agentic Workflows: no supporting signals detected in the sampled repository.
evidence: No agentic/AI-in-CI workflows detected (e.g. AI review bots, LLM steps in CI, auto-merge)
gaps: Below target for an AI-native workflow. Are agents in the loop (review, CI steps, auto-fix/PR, dependency automation)? The high-maturity signal.

dimensions.name: Documentation & Knowledge
dimensions.summary: Documentation & Knowledge: substantial readme; contributing.md; examples directory.
gaps: Below target for an AI-native workflow. Docs for humans and agents: README, /docs, ADRs, changelogs, API docs.

dimensions.name: Code Quality & Guardrails
dimensions.summary: Code Quality & Guardrails: linter configured; formatter configured; typescript strict mode.
evidence: PR review coverage 100% (14 merged · 88% small · 0% reverted)
Pull requests required to merge (1 approval(s) + code owners)
gaps: Already strong — focus on consistency and enforcement.

dimensions.name: Commit & Velocity Signals
dimensions.summary: Commit & Velocity Signals: 30 recent commits analyzed; ai/bot-attributed commits present; some conventional commits.
evidence: AI involved in 8% of PRs (Copilot 2, Claude 1)
gaps: Below target for an AI-native workflow. Process hygiene plus direct evidence of AI in the workflow (AI co-author trailers, conventional commits, cadence).

dimensions.name: AI Process & Harness
dimensions.summary: AI Process & Harness: structured issue templates; default branch protected (6 rules).
gaps: Below target for an AI-native workflow. The operational discipline that makes AI a reliable, repeatable part of development.

dimensions.name: Supply Chain & Security
gaps: Below target for an AI-native workflow. Shift-left security and provenance — the guardrail against the vulnerable, secret-leaking code AI can confidently produce.

headline: vercel/swr sits at L3 — Augmented: AI is part of the standard workflow.
strengths: Automated Testing is a relative strength (100/100).
Code Quality & Guardrails is a relative strength (98/100).
CI/CD & Delivery is a relative strength (75/100).
Commit & Velocity Signals is a relative strength (75/100).

roadmap.title: Agent guidance is thin — agents have little to go on here
roadmap.rationale: AI Tooling & Conventions scored 0/100. Substantive, machine-readable guidance (build/test commands, an architecture map, the rules a change must never break) is what lets an AI contribution land consistently and on-spec. A token stub barely moves trust.
explore: What would an AI agent need to know to make a safe change here — commands, architecture, the constraints it must never break?
Where do new contributors (human or AI) get stuck today for lack of written context?

roadmap.title: AI isn't in the loop yet — it's at most at the keyboard
roadmap.rationale: Agentic Workflows scored 0/100. AI review, auto-fix, and evals for generated output are the jump from 'AI at the keyboard' to a pipeline that can trust an agent's first pass.
explore: Where could an agent take the first pass — review, triage, codegen — with a human just confirming?
What would you need in place to trust an agent's output without reading every line?

roadmap.title: AI use is ad hoc — no shared process or harness
roadmap.rationale: AI Process & Harness scored 21/100. Evals for generated output, versioned prompts/agents, runbooks, and a review gate turn ad-hoc prompting into a repeatable, trustworthy part of how the team ships.
explore: How do you know an AI-generated change is good before it ships — is there an eval or golden test?
Which prompts/agents have worked, and where do they live so the team can reuse them?

warnings: Only part of the repository could be inspected (~45% coverage); treat scores as indicative.
